---
title: "Guarantee Words, Quality Attributes, and Behavioral Adjectives"
---

# Guarantee Words, Quality Attributes, and Behavioral Adjectives

Range: `1421-1512`.

Use the summary table for scanning. Each row links to the detailed anchored entry below.

| Sigil | Term                                  | Summary                                                                                                                                                  |
| ----- | ------------------------------------- | -------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| 1421  | [accessible](#rune-1421)              | A property word; it says what kind of behavior should remain true under stress.                                                                          |
| 1422  | [adaptive](#rune-1422)                | A property word; it says what kind of behavior should remain true under stress.                                                                          |
| 1423  | [amortized](#rune-1423)               | A virtue word; it names an operational quality that can guide design and evaluation.                                                                     |
| 1424  | [approximate {Qual}](#rune-1424)      | A property word; it says what kind of behavior should remain true under stress.                                                                          |
| 1425  | [atomic](#rune-1425)                  | No halfway state is allowed to leak across the line. Use it when partial success would be indistinguishable from corruption.                             |
| 1426  | [auditable](#rune-1426)               | A virtue word; it names an operational quality that can guide design and evaluation.                                                                     |
| 1427  | [authenticated](#rune-1427)           | A virtue word; it names an operational quality that can guide design and evaluation.                                                                     |
| 1428  | [authoritative](#rune-1428)           | A promise adjective; it binds the system to a stronger form of conduct.                                                                                  |
| 1429  | [available](#rune-1429)               | A promise adjective; it binds the system to a stronger form of conduct.                                                                                  |
| 1430  | [backward-compatible](#rune-1430)     | A guarantee word; it claims a behavioral law rather than a mere preference.                                                                              |
| 1431  | [balanced](#rune-1431)                | A property word; it says what kind of behavior should remain true under stress.                                                                          |
| 1432  | [batched](#rune-1432)                 | A virtue word; it names an operational quality that can guide design and evaluation.                                                                     |
| 1433  | [bounded](#rune-1433)                 | A promise adjective; it binds the system to a stronger form of conduct.                                                                                  |
| 1434  | [cache-friendly](#rune-1434)          | A property word; it says what kind of behavior should remain true under stress.                                                                          |
| 1435  | [canonical](#rune-1435)               | A guarantee word; it claims a behavioral law rather than a mere preference.                                                                              |
| 1436  | [causal](#rune-1436)                  | A promise adjective; it binds the system to a stronger form of conduct.                                                                                  |
| 1437  | [composable](#rune-1437)              | A promise adjective; it binds the system to a stronger form of conduct.                                                                                  |
| 1438  | [concurrent](#rune-1438)              | A virtue word; it names an operational quality that can guide design and evaluation.                                                                     |
| 1439  | [configurable](#rune-1439)            | A promise adjective; it binds the system to a stronger form of conduct.                                                                                  |
| 1440  | [consistent](#rune-1440)              | A property word; it says what kind of behavior should remain true under stress.                                                                          |
| 1441  | [convergent](#rune-1441)              | A virtue word; it names an operational quality that can guide design and evaluation.                                                                     |
| 1442  | [crash-safe](#rune-1442)              | A promise adjective; it binds the system to a stronger form of conduct.                                                                                  |
| 1443  | [declarative](#rune-1443)             | A guarantee word; it claims a behavioral law rather than a mere preference.                                                                              |
| 1444  | [deduplicated](#rune-1444)            | A guarantee word; it claims a behavioral law rather than a mere preference.                                                                              |
| 1445  | [deterministic](#rune-1445)           | Given the same inputs, the same result should return. This is the antidote to drift, heisenbugs, and unreviewable behavior.                              |
| 1446  | [durable](#rune-1446)                 | A virtue word; it names an operational quality that can guide design and evaluation.                                                                     |
| 1447  | [eager](#rune-1447)                   | A guarantee word; it claims a behavioral law rather than a mere preference.                                                                              |
| 1448  | [elastic](#rune-1448)                 | A promise adjective; it binds the system to a stronger form of conduct.                                                                                  |
| 1449  | [encrypted](#rune-1449)               | A property word; it says what kind of behavior should remain true under stress.                                                                          |
| 1450  | [eventual](#rune-1450)                | A guarantee word; it claims a behavioral law rather than a mere preference.                                                                              |
| 1451  | [exact](#rune-1451)                   | A promise adjective; it binds the system to a stronger form of conduct.                                                                                  |
| 1452  | [exhaustive](#rune-1452)              | A property word; it says what kind of behavior should remain true under stress.                                                                          |
| 1453  | [extensible](#rune-1453)              | A virtue word; it names an operational quality that can guide design and evaluation.                                                                     |
| 1454  | [fault-tolerant](#rune-1454)          | A resilience word; some breakage is expected and survivable.                                                                                             |
| 1455  | [federated](#rune-1455)               | A guarantee word; it claims a behavioral law rather than a mere preference.                                                                              |
| 1456  | [formal](#rune-1456)                  | A promise adjective; it binds the system to a stronger form of conduct.                                                                                  |
| 1457  | [generic {Qual}](#rune-1457)          | A promise adjective; it binds the system to a stronger form of conduct.                                                                                  |
| 1458  | [hardened](#rune-1458)                | A virtue word; it names an operational quality that can guide design and evaluation.                                                                     |
| 1459  | [hermetic](#rune-1459)                | A promise adjective; it binds the system to a stronger form of conduct.                                                                                  |
| 1460  | [high-availability](#rune-1460)       | A guarantee word; it claims a behavioral law rather than a mere preference.                                                                              |
| 1461  | [idempotent](#rune-1461)              | The word that makes retries survivable: same request, same lasting effect. Shadow: duplicate charges, duplicate emails, and fear of recovery.            |
| 1462  | [immutable](#rune-1462)               | Once written, this thing does not change in place. Immutability buys clarity, replayability, and safer sharing at the cost of update convenience.        |
| 1463  | [incremental](#rune-1463)             | A property word; it says what kind of behavior should remain true under stress.                                                                          |
| 1464  | [indexed](#rune-1464)                 | A promise adjective; it binds the system to a stronger form of conduct.                                                                                  |
| 1465  | [isolated](#rune-1465)                | A promise adjective; it binds the system to a stronger form of conduct.                                                                                  |
| 1466  | [lazy](#rune-1466)                    | A guarantee word; it claims a behavioral law rather than a mere preference.                                                                              |
| 1467  | [linearizable](#rune-1467)            | Every operation behaves as though it took effect at one real instant visible to all observers. Use it rarely, but mean it fully.                         |
| 1468  | [lock-free {Qual}](#rune-1468)        | A progress word; some thread always moves forward without a lock.                                                                                        |
| 1469  | [lossless](#rune-1469)                | A guarantee word; it claims a behavioral law rather than a mere preference.                                                                              |
| 1470  | [lossy](#rune-1470)                   | A property word; it says what kind of behavior should remain true under stress.                                                                          |
| 1471  | [maintainable](#rune-1471)            | A promise adjective; it binds the system to a stronger form of conduct.                                                                                  |
| 1472  | [memory-safe {Qual}](#rune-1472)      | A promise that the system will not casually trespass across allocation boundaries. Shadow: leaks, corruption, and security bugs born from illegal touch. |
| 1473  | [modular](#rune-1473)                 | A guarantee word; it claims a behavioral law rather than a mere preference.                                                                              |
| 1474  | [observable](#rune-1474)              | A promise adjective; it binds the system to a stronger form of conduct.                                                                                  |
| 1475  | [opaque](#rune-1475)                  | A virtue word; it names an operational quality that can guide design and evaluation.                                                                     |
| 1476  | [orthogonal](#rune-1476)              | A promise adjective; it binds the system to a stronger form of conduct.                                                                                  |
| 1477  | [parallel {Qual}](#rune-1477)         | A promise adjective; it binds the system to a stronger form of conduct.                                                                                  |
| 1478  | [portable](#rune-1478)                | A promise adjective; it binds the system to a stronger form of conduct.                                                                                  |
| 1479  | [predictable](#rune-1479)             | A virtue word; it names an operational quality that can guide design and evaluation.                                                                     |
| 1480  | [private](#rune-1480)                 | A virtue word; it names an operational quality that can guide design and evaluation.                                                                     |
| 1481  | [pure](#rune-1481)                    | Same inputs, same output, no hidden state smuggled in from the side. Use it to shrink reasoning scope.                                                   |
| 1482  | [reactive](#rune-1482)                | A virtue word; it names an operational quality that can guide design and evaluation.                                                                     |
| 1483  | [reentrant](#rune-1483)               | A safe-return word; the same code may be entered again before the first invocation finishes.                                                             |
| 1484  | [reliable](#rune-1484)                | A guarantee word; it claims a behavioral law rather than a mere preference.                                                                              |
| 1485  | [reproducible](#rune-1485)            | A guarantee word; it claims a behavioral law rather than a mere preference.                                                                              |
| 1486  | [resilient](#rune-1486)               | A virtue word; it names an operational quality that can guide design and evaluation.                                                                     |
| 1487  | [responsive](#rune-1487)              | A property word; it says what kind of behavior should remain true under stress.                                                                          |
| 1488  | [safe](#rune-1488)                    | A guarded word; some class of failure is explicitly ruled out.                                                                                           |
| 1489  | [sandboxed](#rune-1489)               | A guarantee word; it claims a behavioral law rather than a mere preference.                                                                              |
| 1490  | [scalable](#rune-1490)                | A virtue word; it names an operational quality that can guide design and evaluation.                                                                     |
| 1491  | [secure](#rune-1491)                  | A virtue word; it names an operational quality that can guide design and evaluation.                                                                     |
| 1492  | [serializable {Qual}](#rune-1492)     | Concurrency forced to behave as though transactions happened in one clean order. Expensive when overused, indispensable when history must remain sane.   |
| 1493  | [sharded](#rune-1493)                 | A virtue word; it names an operational quality that can guide design and evaluation.                                                                     |
| 1494  | [side-effect-free {Qual}](#rune-1494) | A property word; it says what kind of behavior should remain true under stress.                                                                          |
| 1495  | [signed](#rune-1495)                  | A property word; it says what kind of behavior should remain true under stress.                                                                          |
| 1496  | [stable](#rune-1496)                  | A virtue word; it names an operational quality that can guide design and evaluation.                                                                     |
| 1497  | [stateful](#rune-1497)                | A guarantee word; it claims a behavioral law rather than a mere preference.                                                                              |
| 1498  | [stateless](#rune-1498)               | A guarantee word; it claims a behavioral law rather than a mere preference.                                                                              |
| 1499  | [streaming](#rune-1499)               | A property word; it says what kind of behavior should remain true under stress.                                                                          |
| 1500  | [strict](#rune-1500)                  | A promise adjective; it binds the system to a stronger form of conduct.                                                                                  |
| 1501  | [synchronized](#rune-1501)            | A guarantee word; it claims a behavioral law rather than a mere preference.                                                                              |
| 1502  | [testable](#rune-1502)                | A guarantee word; it claims a behavioral law rather than a mere preference.                                                                              |
| 1503  | [thread-safe](#rune-1503)             | A guarantee word; it claims a behavioral law rather than a mere preference.                                                                              |
| 1504  | [traceable](#rune-1504)               | A virtue word; it names an operational quality that can guide design and evaluation.                                                                     |
| 1505  | [transactional](#rune-1505)           | A virtue word; it names an operational quality that can guide design and evaluation.                                                                     |
| 1506  | [typed](#rune-1506)                   | A property word; it says what kind of behavior should remain true under stress.                                                                          |
| 1507  | [validated](#rune-1507)               | A property word; it says what kind of behavior should remain true under stress.                                                                          |
| 1508  | [vectorized](#rune-1508)              | A virtue word; it names an operational quality that can guide design and evaluation.                                                                     |
| 1509  | [verified](#rune-1509)                | A property word; it says what kind of behavior should remain true under stress.                                                                          |
| 1510  | [versioned](#rune-1510)               | A virtue word; it names an operational quality that can guide design and evaluation.                                                                     |
| 1511  | [wait-free {Qual}](#rune-1511)        | A guarantee word; it claims a behavioral law rather than a mere preference.                                                                              |
| 1512  | [zero-copy {Qual}](#rune-1512)        | A nearness-and-efficiency word; data moves without redundant duplication.                                                                                |

## Entries

### [1421] accessible {#rune-1421}

**Status:** canonical

**Force:** A property word; it says what kind of behavior should remain true under stress.

### [1422] adaptive {#rune-1422}

**Status:** canonical

**Force:** A property word; it says what kind of behavior should remain true under stress.

### [1423] amortized {#rune-1423}

**Status:** canonical

**Force:** A virtue word; it names an operational quality that can guide design and evaluation.

### [1424] approximate {Qual} {#rune-1424}

**Status:** canonical

**Force:** A property word; it says what kind of behavior should remain true under stress.

### [1425] atomic {#rune-1425}

**Status:** canonical (major canon, pocket canon)

**Force:** No halfway state is allowed to leak across the line. Use it when partial success would be indistinguishable from corruption.

**Pocket gloss:** No halfway state is allowed to leak.

### [1426] auditable {#rune-1426}

**Status:** canonical

**Force:** A virtue word; it names an operational quality that can guide design and evaluation.

### [1427] authenticated {#rune-1427}

**Status:** canonical

**Force:** A virtue word; it names an operational quality that can guide design and evaluation.

### [1428] authoritative {#rune-1428}

**Status:** canonical

**Force:** A promise adjective; it binds the system to a stronger form of conduct.

### [1429] available {#rune-1429}

**Status:** canonical (pocket canon)

**Force:** A promise adjective; it binds the system to a stronger form of conduct.

**Pocket gloss:** Reachable and functioning when needed.

### [1430] backward-compatible {#rune-1430}

**Status:** canonical (pocket canon)

**Force:** A guarantee word; it claims a behavioral law rather than a mere preference.

**Pocket gloss:** New versions do not casually break old clients or data.

### [1431] balanced {#rune-1431}

**Status:** canonical

**Force:** A property word; it says what kind of behavior should remain true under stress.

### [1432] batched {#rune-1432}

**Status:** canonical

**Force:** A virtue word; it names an operational quality that can guide design and evaluation.

### [1433] bounded {#rune-1433}

**Status:** canonical

**Force:** A promise adjective; it binds the system to a stronger form of conduct.

### [1434] cache-friendly {#rune-1434}

**Status:** canonical

**Force:** A property word; it says what kind of behavior should remain true under stress.

### [1435] canonical {#rune-1435}

**Status:** canonical

**Force:** A guarantee word; it claims a behavioral law rather than a mere preference.

### [1436] causal {#rune-1436}

**Status:** canonical

**Force:** A promise adjective; it binds the system to a stronger form of conduct.

### [1437] composable {#rune-1437}

**Status:** canonical (pocket canon)

**Force:** A promise adjective; it binds the system to a stronger form of conduct.

**Pocket gloss:** Small parts combine without excessive friction.

### [1438] concurrent {#rune-1438}

**Status:** canonical

**Force:** A virtue word; it names an operational quality that can guide design and evaluation.

### [1439] configurable {#rune-1439}

**Status:** canonical

**Force:** A promise adjective; it binds the system to a stronger form of conduct.

### [1440] consistent {#rune-1440}

**Status:** canonical (pocket canon)

**Force:** A property word; it says what kind of behavior should remain true under stress.

**Pocket gloss:** Different observers are not being told incompatible stories.

### [1441] convergent {#rune-1441}

**Status:** canonical

**Force:** A virtue word; it names an operational quality that can guide design and evaluation.

### [1442] crash-safe {#rune-1442}

**Status:** canonical (pocket canon)

**Force:** A promise adjective; it binds the system to a stronger form of conduct.

**Pocket gloss:** Failure does not leave storage in nonsense.

### [1443] declarative {#rune-1443}

**Status:** canonical

**Force:** A guarantee word; it claims a behavioral law rather than a mere preference.

### [1444] deduplicated {#rune-1444}

**Status:** canonical

**Force:** A guarantee word; it claims a behavioral law rather than a mere preference.

### [1445] deterministic {#rune-1445}

**Status:** canonical (major canon, pocket canon)

**Force:** Given the same inputs, the same result should return. This is the antidote to drift, heisenbugs, and unreviewable behavior.

**Pocket gloss:** Same inputs, same result.

### [1446] durable {#rune-1446}

**Status:** canonical (pocket canon)

**Force:** A virtue word; it names an operational quality that can guide design and evaluation.

**Pocket gloss:** Once acknowledged, the change really sticks.

### [1447] eager {#rune-1447}

**Status:** canonical

**Force:** A guarantee word; it claims a behavioral law rather than a mere preference.

### [1448] elastic {#rune-1448}

**Status:** canonical

**Force:** A promise adjective; it binds the system to a stronger form of conduct.

### [1449] encrypted {#rune-1449}

**Status:** canonical

**Force:** A property word; it says what kind of behavior should remain true under stress.

### [1450] eventual {#rune-1450}

**Status:** canonical

**Force:** A guarantee word; it claims a behavioral law rather than a mere preference.

### [1451] exact {#rune-1451}

**Status:** canonical

**Force:** A promise adjective; it binds the system to a stronger form of conduct.

### [1452] exhaustive {#rune-1452}

**Status:** canonical

**Force:** A property word; it says what kind of behavior should remain true under stress.

### [1453] extensible {#rune-1453}

**Status:** canonical

**Force:** A virtue word; it names an operational quality that can guide design and evaluation.

### [1454] fault-tolerant {#rune-1454}

**Status:** canonical

**Force:** A resilience word; some breakage is expected and survivable.

### [1455] federated {#rune-1455}

**Status:** canonical

**Force:** A guarantee word; it claims a behavioral law rather than a mere preference.

### [1456] formal {#rune-1456}

**Status:** canonical

**Force:** A promise adjective; it binds the system to a stronger form of conduct.

### [1457] generic {Qual} {#rune-1457}

**Status:** canonical

**Force:** A promise adjective; it binds the system to a stronger form of conduct.

### [1458] hardened {#rune-1458}

**Status:** canonical

**Force:** A virtue word; it names an operational quality that can guide design and evaluation.

### [1459] hermetic {#rune-1459}

**Status:** canonical

**Force:** A promise adjective; it binds the system to a stronger form of conduct.

### [1460] high-availability {#rune-1460}

**Status:** canonical

**Force:** A guarantee word; it claims a behavioral law rather than a mere preference.

### [1461] idempotent {#rune-1461}

**Status:** canonical (major canon, pocket canon)

**Force:** The word that makes retries survivable: same request, same lasting effect.

**Shadow:** duplicate charges, duplicate emails, and fear of recovery.

**Pocket gloss:** Same request, same lasting effect.

### [1462] immutable {#rune-1462}

**Status:** canonical (major canon, pocket canon)

**Force:** Once written, this thing does not change in place. Immutability buys clarity, replayability, and safer sharing at the cost of update convenience.

**Pocket gloss:** Written once, then never changed in place.

### [1463] incremental {#rune-1463}

**Status:** canonical

**Force:** A property word; it says what kind of behavior should remain true under stress.

### [1464] indexed {#rune-1464}

**Status:** canonical

**Force:** A promise adjective; it binds the system to a stronger form of conduct.

### [1465] isolated {#rune-1465}

**Status:** canonical

**Force:** A promise adjective; it binds the system to a stronger form of conduct.

### [1466] lazy {#rune-1466}

**Status:** canonical

**Force:** A guarantee word; it claims a behavioral law rather than a mere preference.

### [1467] linearizable {#rune-1467}

**Status:** canonical (major canon, pocket canon)

**Force:** Every operation behaves as though it took effect at one real instant visible to all observers. Use it rarely, but mean it fully.

**Pocket gloss:** Behaves as though each operation took effect at one visible instant.

### [1468] lock-free {Qual} {#rune-1468}

**Status:** canonical

**Force:** A progress word; some thread always moves forward without a lock.

### [1469] lossless {#rune-1469}

**Status:** canonical

**Force:** A guarantee word; it claims a behavioral law rather than a mere preference.

### [1470] lossy {#rune-1470}

**Status:** canonical

**Force:** A property word; it says what kind of behavior should remain true under stress.

### [1471] maintainable {#rune-1471}

**Status:** canonical (pocket canon)

**Force:** A promise adjective; it binds the system to a stronger form of conduct.

**Pocket gloss:** Cheap enough to understand, modify, and keep healthy.

### [1472] memory-safe {Qual} {#rune-1472}

**Status:** canonical (major canon, pocket canon)

**Force:** A promise that the system will not casually trespass across allocation boundaries.

**Shadow:** leaks, corruption, and security bugs born from illegal touch.

**Pocket gloss:** Will not casually trespass across allocation boundaries.

### [1473] modular {#rune-1473}

**Status:** canonical

**Force:** A guarantee word; it claims a behavioral law rather than a mere preference.

### [1474] observable {#rune-1474}

**Status:** canonical (pocket canon)

**Force:** A promise adjective; it binds the system to a stronger form of conduct.

**Pocket gloss:** Structured so internal behavior can be inferred from external evidence.

### [1475] opaque {#rune-1475}

**Status:** canonical

**Force:** A virtue word; it names an operational quality that can guide design and evaluation.

### [1476] orthogonal {#rune-1476}

**Status:** canonical

**Force:** A promise adjective; it binds the system to a stronger form of conduct.

### [1477] parallel {Qual} {#rune-1477}

**Status:** canonical

**Force:** A promise adjective; it binds the system to a stronger form of conduct.

### [1478] portable {#rune-1478}

**Status:** canonical

**Force:** A promise adjective; it binds the system to a stronger form of conduct.

### [1479] predictable {#rune-1479}

**Status:** canonical

**Force:** A virtue word; it names an operational quality that can guide design and evaluation.

### [1480] private {#rune-1480}

**Status:** canonical

**Force:** A virtue word; it names an operational quality that can guide design and evaluation.

### [1481] pure {#rune-1481}

**Status:** canonical (major canon, pocket canon)

**Force:** Same inputs, same output, no hidden state smuggled in from the side. Use it to shrink reasoning scope.

**Pocket gloss:** Same inputs, same output, no hidden side effects.

### [1482] reactive {#rune-1482}

**Status:** canonical

**Force:** A virtue word; it names an operational quality that can guide design and evaluation.

### [1483] reentrant {#rune-1483}

**Status:** canonical

**Force:** A safe-return word; the same code may be entered again before the first invocation finishes.

### [1484] reliable {#rune-1484}

**Status:** canonical (pocket canon)

**Force:** A guarantee word; it claims a behavioral law rather than a mere preference.

**Pocket gloss:** Keeps doing the promised thing under ordinary stress.

### [1485] reproducible {#rune-1485}

**Status:** canonical (pocket canon)

**Force:** A guarantee word; it claims a behavioral law rather than a mere preference.

**Pocket gloss:** Can be rebuilt or rerun and yield the same result again.

### [1486] resilient {#rune-1486}

**Status:** canonical

**Force:** A virtue word; it names an operational quality that can guide design and evaluation.

### [1487] responsive {#rune-1487}

**Status:** canonical

**Force:** A property word; it says what kind of behavior should remain true under stress.

### [1488] safe {#rune-1488}

**Status:** canonical

**Force:** A guarded word; some class of failure is explicitly ruled out.

### [1489] sandboxed {#rune-1489}

**Status:** canonical

**Force:** A guarantee word; it claims a behavioral law rather than a mere preference.

### [1490] scalable {#rune-1490}

**Status:** canonical (pocket canon)

**Force:** A virtue word; it names an operational quality that can guide design and evaluation.

**Pocket gloss:** Can grow without collapsing the qualities that matter.

### [1491] secure {#rune-1491}

**Status:** canonical

**Force:** A virtue word; it names an operational quality that can guide design and evaluation.

### [1492] serializable {Qual} {#rune-1492}

**Status:** canonical (major canon, pocket canon)

**Force:** Concurrency forced to behave as though transactions happened in one clean order. Expensive when overused, indispensable when history must remain sane.

**Pocket gloss:** Concurrency behaves as though transactions happened in one clean order.

### [1493] sharded {#rune-1493}

**Status:** canonical

**Force:** A virtue word; it names an operational quality that can guide design and evaluation.

### [1494] side-effect-free {Qual} {#rune-1494}

**Status:** canonical

**Force:** A property word; it says what kind of behavior should remain true under stress.

### [1495] signed {#rune-1495}

**Status:** canonical

**Force:** A property word; it says what kind of behavior should remain true under stress.

### [1496] stable {#rune-1496}

**Status:** canonical

**Force:** A virtue word; it names an operational quality that can guide design and evaluation.

### [1497] stateful {#rune-1497}

**Status:** canonical

**Force:** A guarantee word; it claims a behavioral law rather than a mere preference.

### [1498] stateless {#rune-1498}

**Status:** canonical

**Force:** A guarantee word; it claims a behavioral law rather than a mere preference.

### [1499] streaming {#rune-1499}

**Status:** canonical

**Force:** A property word; it says what kind of behavior should remain true under stress.

### [1500] strict {#rune-1500}

**Status:** canonical

**Force:** A promise adjective; it binds the system to a stronger form of conduct.

### [1501] synchronized {#rune-1501}

**Status:** canonical

**Force:** A guarantee word; it claims a behavioral law rather than a mere preference.

### [1502] testable {#rune-1502}

**Status:** canonical

**Force:** A guarantee word; it claims a behavioral law rather than a mere preference.

### [1503] thread-safe {#rune-1503}

**Status:** canonical

**Force:** A guarantee word; it claims a behavioral law rather than a mere preference.

### [1504] traceable {#rune-1504}

**Status:** canonical

**Force:** A virtue word; it names an operational quality that can guide design and evaluation.

### [1505] transactional {#rune-1505}

**Status:** canonical

**Force:** A virtue word; it names an operational quality that can guide design and evaluation.

### [1506] typed {#rune-1506}

**Status:** canonical

**Force:** A property word; it says what kind of behavior should remain true under stress.

### [1507] validated {#rune-1507}

**Status:** canonical

**Force:** A property word; it says what kind of behavior should remain true under stress.

### [1508] vectorized {#rune-1508}

**Status:** canonical

**Force:** A virtue word; it names an operational quality that can guide design and evaluation.

### [1509] verified {#rune-1509}

**Status:** canonical

**Force:** A property word; it says what kind of behavior should remain true under stress.

### [1510] versioned {#rune-1510}

**Status:** canonical

**Force:** A virtue word; it names an operational quality that can guide design and evaluation.

### [1511] wait-free {Qual} {#rune-1511}

**Status:** canonical

**Force:** A guarantee word; it claims a behavioral law rather than a mere preference.

### [1512] zero-copy {Qual} {#rune-1512}

**Status:** canonical

**Force:** A nearness-and-efficiency word; data moves without redundant duplication.
